case_number: Constitution. b. Direct the Respondents No.1

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, MIRPURKHAS C.P. No.D-650 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders on office objection. For hearing of main case. 19.11.2025 Mr. Muhammad Sharif Solangi, Assistant A.G. Sindh. Mr. Muhammad Arif Jhandir advocate files power on behalf of respondent No.1, taken on record. Mr. Abdul Rauf Arain advocate also files power on behalf of respondent No.2, which is taken on record. Petitioners Hussain Bux and others have filed this constitutional petition under Article 199 of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973 seeking the following relief: - a. Declare that the acts and omissions of Respondent No.1 and 2 in not providing the requested information regarding budget, monthly expenditures and development work are illegal, unlawful and violative of the Constitution. b. Direct the Respondents No.1 and 2 to forthwith provide to the petitioners and other residents of UC-5, Town Mir Sher Muhammad, complete record of; i. budget allocated for the area, ii. Details of expenditures incurred; iii. Details of development works undertaken during the past three years. c. Direct the Respondent No.3 and 4 (Province of Sindh and Secretary Local Government) to ensure enforcement of transparency and accountability mechanisms in all Union Councils and Town Committees of Sindh. d. Grant any other relief deemed just and proper in the circumstances of the case. e. Award costs of the petition to the petitioners. At the very outset, learned counsel for the respondents submit that as and when the petitioners would file application for providing information regarding budget, monthly expenditures and development work, the same shall be provided, in accordance with law after hearing the petitioners.

Award costs of the petition to the petitioners. At the very outset, learned counsel for the respondents submit that as and when the petitioners would file application for providing information regarding budget, monthly expenditures and development work, the same shall be provided, in accordance with law after hearing the petitioners. Without touching the merits of the case, the petition stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE JUDGE *Abdullah Channa/PS*
